  "clarification_question": "Which type of help should I prioritize for this section? Please choose one primary option: (A) line-edit for clarity/conciseness and tone (R1.6); (B) validate the argument chain and ensure claims strictly match the reported evidence, adjusting wording if needed (R1.2 + R1.3); (C) reframe the contribution and reorganize the section’s structure (R1.1 + R1.5); (D) assess novelty and citations versus specific prior work like DeepLog/LogAnomaly/LogBERT, updating attribution (R1.4 + R1.7).",
  "rationale": {
    "RX.1": "The section makes strong performance and 'first' claims, so any assistance should surface assumptions, dataset constraints, and uncertainties.",
    "RX.2": "We must avoid overstating results beyond what the reported experiments support, especially for superiority and 'first demonstration' language.",
    "RX.3": "Work should stay within the selected assistance mode and this section’s content without altering experimental design or inventing results.",
    "RX.4": "Edits should not enable misrepresentation of benchmarks or unsafe methodological guidance that could propagate misleading practices.",
    "RX.5": "Maintain clear linkage between claims, numbers, datasets, and cited works so that any changes are traceable to the provided text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which type of help should I prioritize for this section? Please choose one primary option: (A) line-edit for clarity/conciseness and tone (R1.6); (B) validate the argument chain and ensure claims strictly match the reported evidence, adjusting wording if needed (R1.2 + R1.3); (C) reframe the contribution and reorganize the section’s structure (R1.1 + R1.5); (D) assess novelty and citations versus specific prior work like DeepLog/LogAnomaly/LogBERT, updating attribution (R1.4 + R1.7).

rationale.RX.1: The section makes strong performance and 'first' claims, so any assistance should surface assumptions, dataset constraints, and uncertainties.
rationale.RX.2: We must avoid overstating results beyond what the reported experiments support, especially for superiority and 'first demonstration' language.
rationale.RX.3: Work should stay within the selected assistance mode and this section’s content without altering experimental design or inventing results.
rationale.RX.4: Edits should not enable misrepresentation of benchmarks or unsafe methodological guidance that could propagate misleading practices.
rationale.RX.5: Maintain clear linkage between claims, numbers, datasets, and cited works so that any changes are traceable to the provided text.
